etymology.description: 浴(浴) (yù, "bath") refers to taking a bath or shower, often associated with cleansing and hygiene. 巾(巾) (jīn, "towel") is a piece of cloth used for wiping or drying, commonly associated with personal grooming items like towels or handkerchiefs. Together, 浴巾(浴巾) (yùjīn) forms the concept of a bath towel, a specific type of towel used after bathing.

examples.chinese: 我忘记带浴巾(我忘記帶浴巾)。
examples.english: I forgot to bring a bath towel.

examples.chinese: 洗完澡后(洗完澡後)，他用一条白色的浴巾(他用一條白色的浴巾)。
examples.pinyin: Xǐ wán zǎo hòu, tā yòng yī tiáo báisè de yùjīn.
examples.english: After showering, he used a white bath towel.

examples.chinese: 这家酒店提供干净的浴巾(這家酒店提供乾淨的浴巾)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè jiā jiǔdiàn tígōng gānjìng de yùjīn.
examples.english: This hotel provides clean bath towels.
